title: Double Slit Experiment in the Heisenberg Picture of Quantum Mechanics
abstract: We present the standard double slit experiment with non-relativistic particles in the Heisenberg Picture of quantum mechanics. Our motivation is threefold. First and foremost, and contrary to some claims in the literature, we show that there is no need to talk about non-locality when explaining the interference fringes. Secondly, we emphasise the fact that even in the non-relativistic regime, and in order to preserve locality, we should define the position and momentum observables of a particle as functions of both space and time (and not just time). Thirdly, our presentation compares the projective measurements in the Heisenberg picture with the "Church of the Larger Hilbert Space", the latter of which is seldom discussed in the Heisenberg picture of quantum mechanics.
summary: This paper re-examines the double-slit experiment using the Heisenberg picture to argue against the necessity of invoking non-locality. By treating position and momentum as space-time dependent fields, the author shows that interference can be understood through local interactions. The work also explores the 'Church of the Larger Hilbert Space' to describe measurements as local entanglement. Ultimately, it aims to clarify that the apparent 'spooky action' in the Schrödinger picture is an artifact of not treating observables as local fields.
main_problem: The paper addresses the misconception that the double-slit experiment implies non-locality in quantum mechanics.
main_result: The author demonstrates that interference fringes can be fully explained through local interactions by treating position and momentum observables as space-time dependent fields within the Heisenberg picture.
method: The study utilizes the Heisenberg picture of quantum mechanics, employing commutator analysis and the 'Church of the Larger Hilbert Space' to treat measurements as local entanglement between the particle and the screen.
model_or_system: A non-relativistic particle undergoing a standard double-slit experiment, involving free evolution phases and interactions with a screen containing two infinitely narrow slits.
key_observables: Position and momentum operators defined as functions of both space and time (fields).
important_parameters: Slit separation (d), detection position (s), particle mass (m), and time intervals (t1, t2).
main_assumptions: The particle is free during evolution, the slits are infinitely narrow, and the analysis assumes the fundamental validity of locality.
paper_structure: The paper begins by motivating the need to re-examine non-locality, introduces the Heisenberg picture framework, develops the mathematical derivation of interference using space-time dependent observables, compares projective measurements with the 'Church of the Larger Hilbert Space', and concludes with a discussion on the locality of quantum phases.
why_interesting: It provides a fundamental reinterpretation of quantum measurement and locality, which is highly relevant for those working on the foundations of quantum mechanics and the interpretation of entanglement in open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
